moduleTitle: NDT Methods Familiarity: Leak Testing and Liquid Penetrant Testing

lessons.title: Leak Testing Fundamentals and Techniques
objectives: Describe bubble, pressure, halogen detector, and mass spectrometer leak testing fundamentals
Explain LT procedures and techniques including system factors and relative sensitivity
Differentiate evacuated and pressurized system techniques including ambient and tracer fluids, leak locating, and standardization
cp105Topics: 2.3.2.4 Pressurized systems - ambient fluids, tracer fluids

lessons.title: Leak Testing: Interpretation, Safety, Equipment, and Applications
objectives: Summarize LT test result interpretation and essentials of safety
Identify LT test equipment at a familiarity level
Describe LT applications from piping and pressure vessels to hermetic seals and electronic components
cp105Topics: 2.3.6.3 Low-pressure fluid containment vessels, pipes, and tubing

lessons.title: Liquid Penetrant Testing: Fundamentals and Processes
objectives: Explain the interaction of penetrants and discontinuity openings
Describe fluorescence, contrast, and the major penetrant processes
Identify PT test equipment and systems factors
cp105Topics: 2.4.1.1 Interaction of penetrants and discontinuity openings

lessons.title: Liquid Penetrant Testing: Interpretation and Applications
objectives: Interpret PT test results and discontinuity indications at a familiarity level
Describe PT applications including castings, welds, wrought metals, machined parts, leaks, and field inspections
